content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC10650427/", "title": "Influence of Lamination Conditions of EVA Encapsulation on ... - PMC", "summary": "Abstract Encapsulation is a well-known impact factor on the durability of Photovoltaics (PV) modules. Currently there is a lack of understanding on the relationship between lamination process and module durability. In this paper, the effects of different lamination parameters on the encapsulant stability due to stress testing have been investigated from both on-site production quality and long-term stability viewpoints.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["The best range of gel content for the materials tested here is 84\u201390%.", "Samples with gel content below 70% show low chemical and optical stability, weak adhesion strength, and EVA flowing.", "Laminates with gel content over 92% are more likely to become yellow and are less stable in adhesion.", "There are several published studies on lamination conditions, but they only focus on a single material property, e.g., optical transmission, crosslinking degree, or adhesion [7,8,9,10,11,12,13].", "Cross-linking degree can be determined by several different methodologies, ranging from Soxhlet, Raman spectroscopy, differential scanning calorimetry (DSC), rheology, or thermo-mechanical indentation etc. [16,17,18,19,20]."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: The best range of gel content for the materials tested here is 84\u201390%."}, {"url": "https://www.colorado.edu/lab/mcgehee/sites/default/files/attached-files/encapsulating.pdf", "title": "[PDF] Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat and ...", "summary": "## Sustainable Energy & Fuels ## COMMUNICATION Cite this: DOI: 10.1039/c8se00250a Received 30th May 2018 Accepted 3rd July 2018 DOI: 10.1039/c8se00250a rsc.li/sustainable-energy Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) are highly promising, but they are mechanically fragile, composed of layers with mismatches in thermal expansion coe ffi cients, and known to decompose in the presence of heat and moisture. Here we show the development of a glass -glass encapsulation methodology for PSCs that enables them to pass the industry standard IEC 61646 damp heat and thermal cycling tests. It is important to select a thermally stable perovskite composition to withstand the encapsulation process at 150 \u000e C and design a cell that minimizes metal di ff usion.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["## Sustainable Energy & Fuels ## COMMUNICATION Cite this: DOI: 10.1039/c8se00250a Received 30th May 2018 Accepted 3rd July 2018 DOI: 10.1039/c8se00250a rsc.li/sustainable-energy Perovskite solar cells (PSCs) are highly promising, but they are mechani", "Heat or moisture can catalyze the evolution of volatile organic species and leave behind lead iodide. 3 -6 Replacing methylammonium with less volatile cations and using a barrier can enhance thermal and moisture stability. 3,7,8 In addition, PSCs hav", "See DOI: 10.1039/c8se00250a View Article Online View Journal ## Encapsulating perovskite solar cells to withstand damp heat and thermal cycling \u2020 Rongrong Cheacharoen, a Caleb C.", "Some notable previous e ff orts utilized single-layer hydrophobic thin \ue103 lms 8,11 or multi-layer thin \ue103 lm encapsulation from other organic electronic technologies. 12 While the best result of thin \ue103 lm encapsulation has demonstrated a one year lifet", "Glass -glass encapsulation has enabled silicon solar cells to retain 95% PCE a \ue09d er 20 years in the \ue103 eld 17 and is a promising route for PSCs."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: ## Sustainable Energy & Fuels ## COMMUNICATION Cite this: DOI: 10.1039/c8se00250a Received 30th May 2018 Accepted 3rd Ju"}, {"url": "https://2024modulescorecard.pvel.com/damp-heat/", "title": "Damp Heat Test | Kiwa PVEL PV Module Reliability Scorecard", "summary": "This was similar to the 72% reported in last year\u2019s Scorecard, with a median degradation following DH of 1.3% for BOMs produced in 2022 and 1.6% for BOMs produced in 2023. Similar to TC, the number of outliers and failures has also increased. See the Power Degradation graph below for more.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["This was similar to the 72% reported in last year\u2019s Scorecard, with a median degradation following DH of 1.3% for BOMs produced in 2022 and 1.6% for BOMs produced in 2023.", "Key Takeaways Only 69% of BOMs tested degraded by 6%. 11% of BOMs experienced one or more failures during DH testing.", "The DH failure rate was double that reported in the 2023 Scorecard, with 15 manufacturers experiencing at least one failure during DH testing.", "The potential root causes of this could be various cost cutting measures, including the use of POE + EVA encapsulant rather than more traditional POE + POE; a reduction in encapsulant thickness; and/or a reduction in lamination time.", "Power Degradation of DH BOMs Percentage of results shown that are post-BO stabilization: 19% of 2018, 46% of 2019, 95% of 2022, 86% of 2021."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: This was similar to the 72% reported in last year\u2019s Scorecard, with a median degradation following DH of 1.3% for BOMs p"}, {"url": "https://9021fdc6-0afa-473e-b60c-1dbccce51d7c.filesusr.com/ugd/84a06c_290bda798b6949ab879ead6b6b23f93b.pdf?index=true", "title": "[PDF] Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through ...", "summary": "The characteristics of PEVA can be varied by modulating its vinyl acetate (VA) content. According to the general consensus within the PV industry, PEVA as an encapsulant should have VA content b etween 28 and 33%. Degradation of PEVA films due to environmental stressors like humidity, temperature and solar radiation in the field aged modules is not uncommon.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["According to the general consensus within the PV industry, PEVA as an encapsulant should have VA content b etween 28 and 33%.", "PEVA films containing 18, 24, 33, and 40% VA content with t he necessary additives have been fabricated and cured at 150 \u00b0C under vacuum and subsequently subjected to accelerated damp -heat (85\u25e6C and 85% RH) aging according to IEC 61215 standard for ", "Tensile strength, thermal stability and degree of crosslinking have been found to be greater for PEVA containing lower VA content (18 and 24%) and diminished for PEVA with higher VA content (33 and 40%) due to DH aging.", "However, inadequate % transmittance and relatively high stiffness after 2000 h of DH a ging discourages PEVA18 as an encapsulant.", "Moreover, PEVAs with higher VA content (33 and 40%) have been degraded significantly after 2000 h of DH aging r esulting in significant loss in transmittance and mechanical integrity."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: According to the general consensus within the PV industry, PEVA as an encapsulant should have VA content b etween 28 and"}]}
